title: spawn_monitor/1,3 — atomic spawn with monitor
purpose: `erlang:spawn_monitor/1` and `erlang:spawn_monitor/3` atomically spawn a process and establish a monitor — there is no window where the child runs without the monitor. This is the recommended way to spawn monitored processes in OTP. Without it, code must call `spawn` then `monitor` separately, which has a race condition: the child could crash between spawn and monitor, and the DOWN message would be lost.
task: Implement spawn_monitor/1 (fun) and spawn_monitor/3 (MFA) as BIFs that atomically spawn + monitor.

requirements.title: erlang:spawn_monitor/3
requirements.spec: Register `erlang:spawn_monitor/3` accepting (Module, Function, Args). Returns `{Pid, Ref}` tuple.
acceptance: Atomically spawns a new process and establishes a monitor from caller to child
The monitor is established BEFORE the child executes any instructions
If the child crashes, the caller receives `{DOWN, Ref, process, Pid, Reason}`
Test: spawn_monitor a process that crashes — DOWN received with correct Ref

requirements.title: erlang:spawn_monitor/1
requirements.spec: Register `erlang:spawn_monitor/1` accepting a fun (zero-arity closure or MFA export). Returns `{Pid, Ref}`.
acceptance: Accepts a fun term (closure or MFA export)
Spawns and monitors atomically, same semantics as spawn_monitor/3
Test: spawn_monitor with a closure — returns {Pid, Ref}, DOWN received on crash

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement spawn_monitor/4 (with options) — that's spawn_opt territory (B-085)
SHALL NOT allow any execution of the child before the monitor is established
SHALL reuse existing spawn and monitor infrastructure
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
